dailyNews.description: At the centre of the case is whether migrants stopped on the Mexican side of the border can be considered to have “arrived” in the United States under federal law.

dailyNews.title: Democrats slam ICE for using excessive force to arrest woman at San Francisco Airport; 'illegal aliens', says DHS
dailyNews.description: California Democrats criticised ICE after a viral video showed a crying woman being detained at San Francisco airport. dailyNews.title: Newsom's 'American Psycho' comparison to himself backfires in attempt to lampoon Trump
dailyNews.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom drew mockery after comparing himself to "American Psycho" character Patrick Bateman in an apparent parody of President Trump's Elvis post.

dailyNews.title: Former CIA director John Brennan tells MS NOW he believes Iran over President Trump
dailyNews.description: Former CIA Director John Brennan said Monday he believed the Iranian regime over Donald Trump, claiming Trump cannot acknowledge truth when he's "slapped in the face with it."

dailyNews.title: King Charles to address Congress in historic first state visit to Washington
dailyNews.description: King Charles will address Congress next month in his first state visit. The trip follows Trump’s Windsor Castle dinner and Speaker Johnson’s UK address.

dailyNews.title: Who actually runs Iran right now? The key power players as Trump claims talks to 'top' official
dailyNews.description: Analysts say the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has emerged as Iran's dominant force, with Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's grip on power reportedly uncertain after recent strikes.

dailyNews.title: Trump energy czar says Iran conflict gas spike is ‘temporary blip’ as drilling push ramps up
dailyNews.description: Despite the ongoing conflict in Iran, President Donald Trump’s “energy czar" Doug Burgum is confident the “temporary blip up" in gas and energy prices will come back down very soon.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court Seems Open to Trump Request to Block Asylum Seekers at Border
dailyNews.description: A policy of turning back many asylum seekers at the border was rescinded in 2021, but the Justice Department wants the flexibility to reinstate it as a tool for border control.

dailyNews.title: Gregory Bovino’s Final Days: Harsh Words and Few Regrets
dailyNews.description: He was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. But as he begins a retirement that was not entirely voluntary, the Border Patrol leader says he did not go far enough.

dailyNews.title: Costa Rica Agrees to Take Migrants Deported by the Trump Administration
dailyNews.description: The agreement is part of President Trump’s efforts to find governments willing to accept people who have been detained in the United States.

dailyNews.title: Why President Trump Has a Big Oil Problem
dailyNews.description: Our national security correspondent David E. Sanger looks at President Trump’s trouble handling retaliatory attacks by Iran that have largely choked off the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Saudi Leader Is Said to Push Trump to Continue Iran War in Recent Calls
dailyNews.description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman sees a “historic opportunity” to remake the region, according to people briefed by U.S. officials on the conversations.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Threat to ‘Obliterate’ Iran’s Power Stations Could Constitute a War Crime, Rights Experts Say
dailyNews.description: Intentionally targeting the country’s energy infrastructure could constitute a war crime under international law.

dailyNews.title: Trump and Republican National Committee Lean Toward Dallas for Unusual 2026 GOP Midterm Convention
dailyNews.description: Parties normally hold conventions every four years to nominate presidential candidates, but Republicans hope to hold one this year in the face of midterm headwinds.
